reasoning:
Based on the FIDE rankings Wikipedia page as of early November 2025, Fabiano Caruana's classical Elo rating is 2795. To be at least 1% higher, his rating must reach 2823 or greater. 
Since FIDE ratings are only officially updated on the 1st of each month (and Wikipedia follows suit), the rating listed on November 16 will almost certainly be exactly the same as on November 9, meaning a 1% increase is practically impossible (barring Wikipedia vandalism before the date), justifying a probability of 0.02. 
For December 2025 and February 2026, he would have opportunities to update his rating via the December, January, and February lists. However, gaining ~28 Elo points as a top-3 global player is notoriously difficult. At an Elo of 2800, a player needs an extraordinarily long winning streak against elite Grandmasters or to repeatedly crush large fields; drawing or even a single loss can erase the gains from several wins. The empirical base rate for +1% Elo questions provides a strong anchor at 0.007. Even extending out three months, achieving this rating spike is exceedingly unlikely, yielding probabilities of 0.03 and 0.05 to reflect slightly growing uncertainty across time.
